hash_method: SHA-256 of the raw HTTP response bytes fetched with curl -L --compressed on verified_at; an empty hash is never substituted with a URL hash and forces a non-supporting verification status

claims.page_or_section: PDF p.3, organization process and measurement alignment
claims.claim: Enterprise seeding requires cross-functional project organization, aligned measures, and a model refined with first-party, platform, and third-party data.
claims.allowed_use: Define production ownership, measurement fields, and an iterative operating loop.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not infer a visual style, algorithm weight, or fixed KPI threshold.

claims.page_or_section: PDF p.10, feedback and dynamic iteration
claims.claim: User sharing, comments, and interaction can supply feedback for product and experience iteration; content, creator, and exposure touchpoints need coordinated long-term operation.
claims.allowed_use: Require feedback capture and distinguish content, creator, and distribution objects.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not treat comments as representative demand without sampling controls or fabricate comments to create a narrative.

claims.page_or_section: PDF pp.31-32, KFS and content supply matrix
claims.claim: KOL, KOC, and KOS have different content-production and feedback roles; KOC content can reveal selling points, usage scenes, and target groups.
claims.allowed_use: Model creator role, content role, feedback use, and distribution as separate fields.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not copy the report's historical performance ratios or use creator type as a quality guarantee.

claims.page_or_section: PDF pp.31-33, Feeds and Search roles
claims.claim: Feeds and Search serve different decision stages, and paid optimization observes content, audience, and delivery-plan variables.
claims.allowed_use: Separate search/recommendation jobs and paid/natural evidence records.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not describe two natural posts as a randomized A/B test or transfer paid thresholds to organic distribution.

claims.page_or_section: Rendered content-review rules page; safety, content, client, and advertising sections
claims.claim: Commercial content review separates safety, content quality, client requirements, and advertising requirements.
claims.allowed_use: Define separate compliance and quality gates and require delivery consistency.
claims.prohibited_use: Compliance passage does not imply traffic preference or excuse copying third-party content.

claims.page_or_section: Paragraphs 93-102 and 128-134, feedback-to-product loop
claims.claim: The brand describes a repeatable loop from user comments and scene research to product response, public follow-up, and continued iteration, supported by research and R&D systems.
claims.allowed_use: Treat repeated, verifiable response behavior as a possible brand-recognition mechanism and store feedback-to-action links.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not accept self-reported sales causality, invent user requests, or convert 'listening' into staged comments.

claims.page_or_section: Interview section on audience, scene, and content matrices
claims.claim: A mature brand can express one product through multiple audience-scene-content combinations and use different carriers such as education, laboratory visits, and creative narrative.
claims.allowed_use: Generate testable content hypotheses and carrier variants by audience and scene.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not infer that the interview's category conclusions generalize or that any listed carrier is high-performing by default.

claims.page_or_section: PDF abstract and pp.1-4, stakeholder negotiation and obscured labor
claims.claim: KOC content production is negotiated among creators, marketers, brands, and the platform; commercial labor may be deliberately obscured to appear spontaneous.
claims.allowed_use: Preserve creator voice and everyday material while making sponsorship and production labor auditable.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not use 'native feel' to justify hidden sponsorship, fake ordinary users, or uncompensated creator labor.

claims.page_or_section: PDF pp.6-8, centralized distribution and 1:1 replication failure modes
claims.claim: Distributing centralized brand material can become preachy and staged, while direct replication of already-validated seeding paths favors short-term extraction over durable differentiation.
claims.allowed_use: Create anti-patterns for ad-like material dumping and blind template replication.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not turn the agency model into a universal platform law or a per-post fixed structure.

claims.page_or_section: PDF pp.10-12, WHY-HOW-WHAT narrative axis
claims.claim: The report proposes value viewpoint, dynamic scene experience, and user identity as a strategic narrative axis across content, search, and browse surfaces.
claims.allowed_use: Use as a brief-level strategy prompt and source of hypotheses for different surfaces.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not force WHY-HOW-WHAT into every post or claim its effectiveness without live evidence.

claims.page_or_section: Section 3, more scientific content planning and production
claims.claim: A cross-functional creative-at-scale team tagged material elements, tested audience attention, evaluated delivery, and optimized material for subsequent rounds.
claims.allowed_use: Store element tags, audience hypothesis, version, evaluation result, and reinvestment decision in the content system.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not transfer the reported eye-tracking result, lift, or audience-specific creative conclusion to Xiaohongshu posts.

claims.page_or_section: Sections 1-2, trend insight and audience-product-scene matrix
claims.claim: The case starts from audience overlap and a differentiated efficacy scene, then restructures the message house and audience-product-scene content matrix.
claims.allowed_use: Require a stated audience, scene, message pillar, and differentiation hypothesis before production.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not accept the case's claimed growth as causal evidence or copy its audience/category conclusion.

claims.page_or_section: Sections 3-4 and award-submission strategy, high-TI content and search-feed coordination
claims.claim: The case identifies higher-decision-flow posts, studies their keyword/search paths, and uses search and feed surfaces for different stages and later delivery.
claims.allowed_use: Record why a post is selected for further delivery and preserve keyword, surface, and audience-flow context.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not use AIPS/TI language as an organic algorithm formula or quote unverified scale claims as thresholds.

claims.page_or_section: Sections on KOL operations, UGC, and enterprise-account operations
claims.claim: The service workflow monitored trends, creators, competitors, and sentiment, adapted product benefits to creator style, and gave UGC and enterprise accounts different jobs.
claims.allowed_use: Separate monitoring, creator adaptation, UGC collection, and enterprise-account workstreams.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not copy reported metrics, rely on 2021 channel tactics, hide commercial disclosure, or manufacture UGC.

claims.page_or_section: PDF pp.11-12, scientific commercialization and value alignment
claims.claim: The case study treats track insight, target setting, delivery optimization, attribution, method, and commercial products as a connected operating system rather than isolated traffic tactics.
claims.allowed_use: Define system boundaries and require traceability from insight through outcome.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not infer visual or copywriting performance from a business-model case study.

claims.page_or_section: Public report page, audience segmentation and people-scene-motive sections
claims.claim: The joint study segments health audiences by label, scene, and motive, and distinguishes functional, efficiency, social, and meaning motives that translate product functions into perceivable outcomes.
claims.allowed_use: Require a brief to record functional need, lived scene, motive, and perceivable outcome as separate fields and generate different hypotheses for each motive.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not generalize the health segments to other categories, infer a visual style or traffic weight, or quote public-page figures without their survey context.

claims.page_or_section: Team structure, role collaboration, project board, and small-team review sections
claims.claim: The guide assigns business ownership to operations, separates content, delivery, supply, and support roles, and recommends tracking each SKU, content asset, and delivery plan with explicit owners and review responsibilities.
claims.allowed_use: Define project ownership, reviewer independence, SKU-content-delivery links, and problem localization fields.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not claim the proposed team structure causes traffic or treat unsourced editorial guidance as an industry benchmark.

claims.page_or_section: Brief translation, creator selection, production response, monitoring, and retrospective sections
claims.claim: The agency describes translating brand language into audience pain, scene, and perceivable benefit, checking creator history and recent query context, assigning specialist roles, and returning communication, cost, and conversion data to a knowledge base.
claims.allowed_use: Require a brand-to-user translation trace, creator-history evidence, current query snapshot, specialist review, and separated outcome layers.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not accept the agency's self-reported hit rate, manufacture comments or testimonials, automate engagement, or infer causality from client promotional material.

claims.page_or_section: Execution granularity, judgment externalization, and transfer sections
claims.claim: The practitioner argues that an operational SOP must externalize why a pattern works, what qualifies as acceptable, how it transfers across audience, scene, emotion, and product, and how failures are diagnosed instead of merely listing posting actions.
claims.allowed_use: Use as a schema and workflow prompt for mechanism, scope, contraindication, counterexample, acceptance criteria, and rejection reason.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not use the article as evidence that a content mechanism performs or interpret structural transfer as close copying of an original post.

claims.page_or_section: Creative-center and scale-distribution organization sections
claims.claim: The practitioner separates zero-to-one content-model exploration from one-to-many production, notes that content models expire, and varies account, scene, angle, material, and expression only after a model is tested.
claims.allowed_use: Define candidate, validated, reusable, expired, and downgraded lifecycle states and keep exploratory tests separate from production variants.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not treat the proposed organization as universal, count bulk generation as validation, or indefinitely replicate one high-performing model.

claims.page_or_section: Public show notes on content middle-platform exploration, production, data return, and replication
claims.claim: The show notes frame industrial content operations as direction exploration, stable material production, data return, and scale using variable audience, scene, pain, function, and emotion fields, while also promoting several unverified or risky tactics.
claims.allowed_use: Use only as a pressure test for workflow completeness and a source of explicit anti-patterns.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not use covert-camera framing, masked pseudo-user presentation, staged supplier comments, cheap mass posting, unverified ROI, or a fixed reading threshold as production rules.

claims.page_or_section: Whitepaper origin, eight capability chapters, risk controls, and four operating pillars
claims.claim: A Xiaohongshu channel-business whitepaper co-created with sixteen agencies structures partner operations across strategy, client development, strategy, content, media delivery, service, team, and business management, and describes productized strategy, standardized operations, data assets, and organized talent as reusable operating capabilities.
claims.allowed_use: Define separate production and governance objects, ownership, operating gates, and risk records across the content lifecycle.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not infer recommendation-algorithm weights, style-performance effects, or universal agency organization from a partner-management whitepaper.

claims.page_or_section: Content-team structure, topic-to-review-to-retrospective SOP, tools, and role matrix
claims.claim: A coauthor of the whitepaper's content chapter publicly describes a topic-demand, creation, review, and retrospective pipeline with explicit role, material, tool, and follow-up records.
claims.allowed_use: Require traceable topic evidence, framework, source material, review, retrospective window, and next-action fields, while separating search, competitive, delivery, and outcome data.
claims.prohibited_use: Do not reuse its self-reported daily output, hit-rate, review-time, creator, or cost thresholds as defaults or performance evidence.

product_decisions.decision: Store observable mechanism, scope, evidence, and boundary rather than subjective decoration labels alone.
One draft has one primary content job and is evaluated by the matching funnel stage.
Produce at least two prototypes with different attention paths; color-only variants do not count.
Review every prototype both in feed-thumbnail size and full size.
Native adaptation never permits hidden sponsorship, fake identity, or fabricated personal experience.
A repeated, verifiable brand action may be used as a recognition device; logo size is not the only brand-control lever.
Every content-matrix row stores one audience-scene-message-carrier hypothesis and one actively varied factor.
A style association remains candidate until it has recomputable same-metric baselines, independent accounts and clusters, query context, and counterevidence.
Natural-post comparisons are quasi-experimental observations; paid tests require audience, budget, delivery, time, and variable logs.
Evidence, native fit, visual quality, factual/compliance, client, delivery, and post-publication gates are recorded separately.
Evaluation metrics are selected from the content's primary job and surface rather than one global engagement-rate threshold.
The system retains source, hypothesis, version, review, delivery conditions, outcome, counterevidence, and selection/rejection reason.
A production brief records functional need, lived scene, motive, and perceivable outcome separately before it selects a carrier or aesthetic direction.
Each SKU-content-delivery unit has an explicit owner and an independent reviewer for material strategic or budget decisions.
Exploratory content models and scaled production variants are separate objects; models can expire or be downgraded and bulk generation never counts as validation.
Industrialization scales evidence capture, material versioning, review, and learning; it does not scale fabricated native identities, covert capture, staged comments, or pseudo-precise thresholds.
